---
title: "🗓️ Lecture 01: General introduction"
date: "2026-09-14"
date-modified: last-modified
---

## 🎯 Content and goals

- We get to know each other and clarify our goals and interests in the topics of the course.
- You are given a general overview of the course, the kind of examination, the role of the take-home exams, and the rules for using AI.
- We introduce a set of general software tools that we recommend you use (e.g. for literature management or note-taking).

## 🖥️ Lecture Slides

*The slides for this lecture will be made available here.*

<!-- When a slide PDF is ready, place it in `content/lecture/slides/`
and embed it with the block below (adjust the filename):

```{=html}
<div class="container" style="position: relative;overflow: hidden;width: 100%;padding-top: 56.25%;">
<iframe src="slides/RM26_L01_Introduction.pdf" style="position: absolute;top: 0;left: 0;bottom: 0;right: 0;width: 100%;height: 100%;" allowfullscreen loading="lazy"></iframe>
</div>
```
-->

## 📚 Mandatory Reading

*No mandatory reading for this lecture.*

## 🏆 Further Readings

- *For note-taking with Obsidian:* @miller2023obsidian
- *For writing text with Markdown:* @cone2025markdown; @commonmark
- *For bibliographic management with Zotero:* @zoteroquickstart
- *On using LLMs such as Claude:* @kuka2024prompting

## ✍️ Exercises and coursework

*Suggested exercise (to help you remember the material; not graded):*

- Familiarize yourself with Moodle, the course webpage, and the previous knowledge in mathematics and statistics required for the course.

-------

If you have questions or problems, please post them in the [Moodle](https://elearning.uni-flensburg.de/moodle/course/view.php?id=17920) forum.

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
